To address this issue, we propose Adaptive Co-optimization via Empirical Credit Assignment for Text-to-SQL (ACE-SQL), a reinforcement learning (RL) framework that jointly optimizes schema retrieval and SQL generation under execution feedback. ACE-SQL constructs an online column-set pool from generator rollouts and derives adaptive on-policy retrieval targets from the column set most frequently associated with execution-correct rollouts. This induces bidirectional adaptation, where the retriever adapts toward column sets that the generator can execute correctly, while the generator adapts to the retriever's evolving schema selections under execution feedback. With approximately 3k synthetic Text-to-SQL question-database pairs for RL training, ACE-SQL achieves 65.3% greedy execution accuracy on BIRD Dev while using 0.93k output tokens per query. The repository is available at https://github.com/xbchen1/ACE-SQL.
title: ACE-SQL: Adaptive Co-Optimization via Empirical Credit Assignment for Text-to-SQL
title_translated: ACE-SQL：基于经验信用分配的自适应协同优化用于文本到SQL
summary_translated: 文本到SQL任务将自然语言问题映射为可执行的SQL查询。现代数据库通常包含大型复杂模式，使得模式链接成为准确生成SQL的关键步骤。现有方法要么依赖全模式生成，使模式链接隐含在庞大的搜索空间中；要么使用独立的检索器，以静态真实列监督进行训练，但其目标可能并非当前生成器策略的最优选择。为解决此问题，我们提出ACE-SQL——一种基于执行反馈的强化学习（RL）框架，联合优化模式检索与SQL生成。ACE-SQL从生成器的回放中构建在线列集池，并从与执行正确回放最频繁关联的列集中推导出自适应的在线策略检索目标。这实现了双向适应：检索器向生成器能正确执行的列集调整，同时生成器也根据检索器不断演化的模式选择在执行反馈下进行适应。在约3000个合成的文本到SQL问答-数据库对上进行RL训练，ACE-SQL在BIRD Dev集上达到65.3%的贪婪执行准确率，每查询仅使用0.93k输出token。代码仓库地址：https://github.com/xbchen1/ACE-SQL。

summary: Large language models (LLMs) have fundamentally transformed the landscape of Natural Language Processing. Despite these advances, LLMs and LLM-based systems remain prone to a variety of failure modes. Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems have emerged as a common deployment scenario seeking to both avoid the well known risk of the LLM "hallucinating" information, and to enable reasoning and question answering over proprietary information that the LLM did not have access to during training without resorting to expensive model fine-tuning.   In this work, we explore the idea of using a lightweight graph structure with a relatively simple graph schema, to support the RAG subsystem via a dedicated toolset. We design an agentic system with a variety of vector search and graph query tools operating over a structured dataset based on a curated subset of English Wikipedia articles, and evaluate its performance on questions from MoNaCo, a challenging Wikipedia QA benchmark of complex query answering tasks.   Our results show that the introduction of graph-based tools can significantly increase the precision and recall of factual correctness, can halve the number of hallucinated answers, and achieves the highest fine-grained truthfulness score among the three evaluated scenarios. All this with a modest increase in token usage.
title: Reducing Hallucinations in Complex Question Answering using Simple Graph-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (long version)
title_translated: 通过简单图结构检索增强生成减少复杂问答中的幻觉（长版）
summary_translated: 大型语言模型（LLMs）已彻底改变自然语言处理的格局。尽管取得进展，LLMs及其系统仍易出现多种失效模式。检索增强生成（RAG）系统已成为常见部署方案，旨在避免LLM‘幻觉’信息的风险，并在不进行昂贵模型微调的前提下，实现对训练期间未接触过的专有信息的推理与问答。在本研究中，我们探索使用轻量级图结构及相对简单的图模式，通过专用工具集支持RAG子系统。我们设计了一个智能体系统，配备多种向量搜索与图查询工具，作用于基于精选英文维基百科文章子集的结构化数据集，并在MoNaCo这一具有挑战性的维基百科问答基准（复杂查询任务）上评估其性能。结果表明，引入基于图的工具可显著提高事实正确性的精确率与召回率，使幻觉答案数量减半，并在细粒度真实性评分上超越其他两种评估场景。所有这些改进仅带来适度的token消耗增加。

summary: Research attention is widely used as an indicator of visibility, influence, and societal uptake, yet it is typically represented as aggregated counts that do not preserve how attention develops across contexts over time. This creates a mismatch between how attention is interpreted and how it is represented. We propose attention flows as contextually structured representations that encode the organisation of attention and its evolution over time. We evaluate whether these representations capture transferable structure by constructing a benchmark based on analogy-style reasoning across research outputs. Comparing signal, sequence, and flow-based representations, we find that flow representations more effectively support structural comparison, particularly in settings where attention is shaped by temporal progression or context distributions. We further show that learned flow representations improve robustness under partial observation and structural perturbation. Overall, these results support modelling attention as a contextually structured phenomenon and provide a basis for more informative approaches to research evaluation.
title: Representing Research Attention as Contextually Structured Flows
comment: Accepted at STi 2026 - International Conference on Science and Technology Indicators
title_translated: 将研究关注度表示为上下文结构化的流动
summary_translated: 研究关注度广泛用作可见性、影响力和社会采纳的指标，但通常以聚合计数形式表示，无法保留注意力在时间与上下文中的演变过程。这造成了注意力解读方式与其表示方式之间的不匹配。我们提出‘注意力流’作为上下文结构化的表示形式，编码注意力的组织结构及其随时间的演化。我们通过构建基于类比推理的研究成果基准，评估这些表示是否具备可迁移的结构特性。对比信号、序列与流式表示，我们发现流式表示在注意力受时间推进或上下文分布影响的场景中更有效地支持结构比较。此外，我们还证明学习到的流表示在部分观测和结构扰动下具有更强的鲁棒性。总体而言，这些结果支持将注意力建模为上下文结构化现象，并为更富信息量的研究评价方法提供了基础。

summary: Long-horizon agents can archive large histories, but future answers still incur retrieval, rereading, and context costs. When retained memory misses answer-relevant evidence, the system must return to larger portions of the raw history. We study budgeted evidence survival: before the query is known, which source evidence should be retained so that it remains recoverable and usable under a fixed retained source-evidence token budget? We instantiate this setting as Budgeted Pre-Query Retention, where memory is written during ingestion and later read without access to the full raw stream. We introduce EMBER, a learned retention policy that constructs a compact, source-backed evidence state. EMBER stores evidence capsules: verbatim source excerpts paired with retrieval keys and update metadata, preserving both grounding and read-time access. Post-query outcome feedback trains the writer to preserve evidence across the ingestion-retrieval-answer chain. On LongMemEval-RR, our LongMemEval-derived retained-evidence protocol, EMBER-14B reaches 0.3017 F1 at the 8192-token retained-evidence comparison point, compared with 0.1765 for the strongest non-EMBER budgeted baseline. Across retained source-evidence budgets, EMBER improves F1, Retain-Recall, and Read-Recall, indicating that long-horizon memory depends on retaining evidence within the budget rather than rereading larger histories.
title: EMBER: Efficient Memory via Budgeted Evidence Retention for Long-Horizon Agents
title_translated: EMBER：通过预算化证据保留实现长时程智能体的高效记忆
summary_translated: 长时程智能体可以存储大量历史信息，但未来回答仍需付出检索、重读和上下文成本。当保留的记忆中缺少与答案相关的关键证据时，系统必须返回到更大的原始历史片段。我们研究了预算化证据留存问题：在查询未知之前，应保留哪些来源证据，以确保其在固定保留证据token预算下仍可恢复并使用？我们将此设定实例化为预算化预查询保留机制，其中记忆在数据摄入阶段写入，后续读取时不访问完整原始流。我们提出了EMBER，一种学习型保留策略，构建紧凑且基于来源的证据状态。EMBER存储证据胶囊：原文摘录配以检索键和更新元数据，既保留了事实依据，又保证了读取时的可访问性。通过查询后的结果反馈训练写入者在整个摄入-检索-回答链中保留关键证据。在LongMemEval-RR上，我们基于LongMemEval设计的保留证据协议中，EMBER-14B在8192 token保留证据对比点达到0.3017的F1分数，远超最强非EMBER预算基线的0.1765。在不同保留来源证据预算下，EMBER均提升了F1、保留召回率和读取召回率，表明长时程记忆依赖于在预算内保留证据，而非重复阅读更大历史。

summary: LLMs are increasingly deployed as Artificial Moral Advisors (AMA) in a variety of contexts: what kind of conversational patterns should they display? In this paper, we study how AMA can help their interlocutors "stay with the uncertainty". We propose three modes of uncertainty (Perspective-Multiplying, Tension-Preserving, Process-Reflecting) and compare them against three control conditions (Baseline, Persuasive, Sycophantic). A user-agent LLM engages in a dialogue on an ethical dilemma with an AMA following a specific uncertainty strategy, and completes pre- and post-conversation questionnaires. We further examine the effect of two persona prompt formats (Declarative and Narrative). We found that (1) no single model dominates as a simulated user agent, with open models aligning with human ambiguity through between-persona divergence and closed models through within-persona hedging; (2) declarative personas better capture initial stance diversity while narrative personas show more realistic belief revision; (3) all six AMA strategies produce distinguishable conversational patterns; and (4) uncertainty strategies differ not in how much stance revision they produce, but in the quality of engagement they sustain.
title: Staying with the Uncertainty: Uncertainty-Scaffolding Strategies for Artificial Moral Advisors in LLM-to-LLM Simulated Conversations
title_translated: 保持不确定性：大语言模型间模拟对话中人工道德顾问的不确定性支撑策略
summary_translated: 大语言模型（LLMs）正被广泛部署为人工道德顾问（AMA），但在不同情境下应展现何种对话模式？本文研究AMA如何帮助对话对象“持续面对不确定性”。我们提出三种不确定性处理模式（视角多元化、张力维持、过程反思），并与三种对照条件（基础、说服性、奉承性）进行比较。一个用户代理LLM与遵循特定不确定性策略的AMA就伦理困境展开对话，并完成对话前后问卷调查。我们进一步考察两种角色提示格式（陈述式与叙事式）的影响。研究发现：(1) 没有单一模型能作为理想的模拟用户代理，开放模型通过跨角色差异体现人类模糊性，封闭模型则通过角色内保守表达体现；(2) 陈述式角色更准确捕捉初始立场多样性，而叙事式角色表现出更真实的信念修正；(3) 六种AMA策略均产生可区分的对话模式；(4) 不同不确定性策略并非在引发多少立场改变上存在差异，而在于其维持高质量互动的能力。

summary: Current evaluations for Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) overwhelmingly focus on utility-driven objectives, leaving model behavior under logic-neutral scenarios largely underexplored. Stochasticity is essential in scenarios where multiple actions are equally valid, such as recommending travel itineraries or daily schedules where multiple options have similar utility. In such settings, deterministic policies may lead to repetitive behaviors and reduced coverage of valid alternatives. To bridge this gap, we propose RandomBench, a benchmark designed to evaluate whether MLLMs can maintain distributionally neutral behavior when selecting among equivalent options. We further introduce three metrics, including RI, BCI, BII, to quantify entropy and distributional bias. Experiments reveal a pervasive phenomenon termed Stochastic Collapse, where MLLMs fail to maintain uniform randomness under explicit random instructions, with top-1 probabilities reaching 97% from the ideal one quarter baseline and RI dropping to 0.068 in Claude Sonnet 4.6. Extensive ablation studies further demonstrate that these deviations persist across languages and representation formats, highlighting the robustness of distributional collapse in logic-neutral decision settings.
title: Evaluating Stochastic Collapse and Implicit Bias in Multimodal Large Language Models
title_translated: 多模态大语言模型中随机性坍塌与隐含偏见的评估
summary_translated: 当前对多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）的评估主要聚焦于实用性目标，导致在逻辑中立场景下的模型行为仍缺乏充分探索。在多个动作同样合理的情境中（如推荐旅行路线或日程安排），随机性至关重要，因为确定性策略可能导致重复行为并减少有效选项的覆盖范围。为填补这一空白，我们提出RandomBench基准，用于评估MLLMs在选择等效选项时是否能保持分布中立的行为。我们进一步引入RI、BCI、BII三项指标，量化熵值与分布偏差。实验揭示了一种普遍现象——随机性坍塌：即使在明确的随机指令下，MLLMs也难以维持均匀随机性，顶级选项概率高达97%（理想应为25%），RI下降至Claude Sonnet 4.6中的0.068。广泛的消融研究进一步表明，这些偏差在不同语言和表示格式中均持续存在，凸显了逻辑中立决策场景下分布坍塌的鲁棒性。

summary: Large language models (LLMs) drive significant financial innovations, yet their high-concurrency deployment is severely bottlenecked by KV cache memory overhead, which inflates infrastructure costs and throttles scalability. To address this, we propose YouZhi-LLM, a highly efficient financial LLM empowered by a comprehensive structural transition and training pipeline natively built on the Huawei Ascend ecosystem. At its algorithmic core, YouZhi-LLM features a layer-adaptive GQA-to-MLA transition framework that dynamically assigns per-layer FreqFold sizes, maximizing KV-cache compression while minimizing perplexity degradation. To recover representation capacity and inject domain expertise, the Ascend-based training pipeline seamlessly integrates generalized knowledge distillation with financial-specific supervised fine-tuning. Evaluations demonstrate the superiority of this systematic approach, with the adaptive transition reducing perplexity degradation by up to 35% over uniform baselines. Crucially, when evaluated on Ascend NPUs via vLLM-Ascend, the massive KV-cache reduction translates directly into deployment efficiency. Compared to their respective base models, YouZhi-7B yields a 12.3% improvement in average financial benchmark score alongside a 2.69$\times$ increase in maximum concurrency; similarly, YouZhi-14B achieves a 7.0% accuracy gain and a 2.43$\times$ concurrency boost, establishing a new paradigm for cost-effective, high-throughput financial inference.
title: YouZhi: Towards High-Concurrency Financial LLMs via Adaptive GQA-to-MLA Transition
title_translated: YouZhi：通过自适应GQA到MLA转换实现高并发金融大语言模型
summary_translated: 大语言模型（LLMs）推动了显著的金融创新，但其高并发部署严重受限于KV缓存内存开销，导致基础设施成本上升并制约可扩展性。为此，我们提出YouZhi-LLM，一种基于华为Ascend生态原生构建的高效金融LLM，包含全面的结构转换与训练流程。其核心算法是层自适应GQA到MLA转换框架，动态分配每层的FreqFold大小，在最大化KV缓存压缩的同时最小化困惑度退化。为恢复表征能力并注入领域专长，基于Ascend的训练流程无缝整合广义知识蒸馏与金融特定监督微调。评估表明该系统方法具有显著优势，自适应转换相比均匀基线将困惑度退化降低高达35%。尤为重要的是，在Ascend NPUs上通过vLLM-Ascend评估时，巨大的KV缓存减少直接转化为部署效率提升。相较于各自基线模型，YouZhi-7B在平均金融基准得分上提升12.3%，最大并发量提高2.69倍；YouZhi-14B则实现7.0%的准确率提升和2.43倍的并发增强，确立了低成本、高吞吐金融推理的新范式。

summary: We investigate the extent to which the language processing of LLMs resembles human cognitive processes, focusing on a human cognitive bias called the $\textit{neglect-zero effect}$. This effect refers to the human tendency to ignore $\textit{zero-models}$, which are configurations that render a proposition vacuously true by virtue of an empty set. We focus on two types of inferences driven by the neglect-zero effect, and examine how LLMs process these inferences by comparing their behavior with that in an inference that does not involve the neglect-zero effect. For this purpose, we employ a paradigm based on $\textit{structural priming}$, where recent exposure to a preceding sentence (the $\textit{prime}$) facilitates the processing of a subsequent sentence (the $\textit{target}$) due to their structural similarity. We prepare primes to force LLMs to consider the zero-model, and analyze whether they also consider it in the target. The results suggest that the neglect-zero effect may not occur in the LLMs analyzed in this study. Our code is available at https://github.com/ynklab/neglect_zero
title: Analysis of the Neglect-Zero Effect in Large Language Models
comment: 14 pages (10 pages main text), 8 figures. To appear in the Proceedings of the ACL2026 Student Research Workshop (SRW)
title_translated: 大语言模型中忽略零效应的分析
summary_translated: 我们探讨大语言模型（LLMs）的语言处理是否类似于人类认知过程，重点关注一种称为“忽略零效应”的人类认知偏差。该效应指人类倾向于忽略“零模型”——即通过空集使命题在逻辑上平凡成立的配置。我们聚焦由忽略零效应驱动的两类推理，并通过对比不涉及该效应的推理，考察LLMs如何处理这些推理。为此，我们采用基于“结构启动”的范式，即近期接触的前一句（“启动句”）因与后续句（“目标句”）结构相似而促进其处理。我们设计启动句迫使LLMs考虑零模型，并分析其在目标句中是否也予以考虑。结果表明，在本研究分析的LLMs中，忽略零效应可能并未出现。相关代码已公开于https://github.com/ynklab/neglect_zero。

summary: Latent reasoning has emerged as a promising alternative to discrete Chain-of-Thought (CoT) in large language models (LLMs), enabling more expressive reasoning by operating over continuous representations. However, the inherently deterministic nature of continuous representations limits policy exploration in reinforcement learning (RL). To address this, we propose TARPO (Token-Wise Latent-Explicit Reasoning via Action-Routing Policy Optimization), a pure RL framework that adaptively switches between discrete token generation and continuous latent reasoning at each step. TARPO introduces a lightweight action head router that observes the current hidden state and samples a routing decision from a binary mode-selection space, preserving the stochasticity of discrete token sampling from the vocabulary. The LLM backbone and router are jointly optimized end-to-end with a shared group-relative advantage signal. Extensive experiments across Qwen2.5 (from 1.5B to 7B) and Llama-3.1-8B backbones demonstrate that TARPO consistently outperforms existing explicit and latent reasoning RL baselines across diverse benchmarks. Further analysis shows that TARPO learns adaptive token-wise switching behaviors while maintaining stable training dynamics. Our code is available at https://github.com/NKU-LITI/TARPO-master.
title: TARPO: Token-Wise Latent-Explicit Reasoning via Action-Routing Policy Optimization
comment: 18 pages, 12 figures. Code available at this https URL
title_translated: TARPO：通过动作路由策略优化实现逐标记的隐式显式推理
summary_translated: 隐式推理已成为大型语言模型（LLMs）中替代离散思维链（CoT）的一种有前景的方法，通过在连续表示上操作实现更丰富的推理。然而，连续表示固有的确定性限制了强化学习（RL）中的策略探索。为此，我们提出TARPO（基于动作路由策略优化的逐标记隐式显式推理），一个纯RL框架，能够在每一步自适应地在离散标记生成和连续隐式推理之间切换。TARPO引入了一个轻量级的动作头路由器，该路由器观察当前隐藏状态，并从二元模式选择空间中采样路由决策，从而保留了从词汇表中进行离散标记采样的随机性。LLM主干网络与路由器通过共享的组相对优势信号端到端联合优化。在Qwen2.5（1.5B至7B）和Llama-3.1-8B主干网络上的大量实验表明，TARPO在多种基准测试中持续优于现有的显式和隐式推理RL基线。进一步分析显示，TARPO学会了自适应的逐标记切换行为，同时保持了稳定的训练动态。代码已公开于https://github.com/NKU-LITI/TARPO-master。

summary: Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) have opened new avenues for generating training-free text embeddings. However, the causal attention in decoder-only LLMs prevents earlier tokens from attending to future context, leading to biased contextualized representations. In this work, we propose Reverse prompting with Explicit One-word Limitation (ReverseEOL), a simple yet effective method for enhancing the representational capability of frozen LLMs. ReverseEOL augments the standard forward embedding with an additional reversed embedding derived from the reversed input text. Since reversing the input exposes each token to context inaccessible in the original order, the resulting reversed embedding effectively provides complementary information to the original one. As a result, combining the forward and reversed embeddings yields a richer final representation. Comprehensive experiments on STS and MTEB benchmarks demonstrate that ReverseEOL significantly improves the performance of existing training-free baselines across a broad range of LLMs with diverse architectures and scales. Extensive ablations and analyses further confirm the necessity of our reversal mechanism.
title: ReverseEOL: Improving Training-free Text Embeddings via Text Reversal in Decoder-only LLMs
title_translated: ReverseEOL：通过解码器-only LLM 中的文本反转提升无训练文本嵌入
summary_translated: 大型语言模型（LLMs）的最新进展为生成无训练文本嵌入开辟了新途径。然而，解码器-only LLM 中的因果注意力机制阻止早期标记关注未来上下文，导致上下文表示存在偏差。本文提出了一种简单而有效的方法——带显式单字限制的反向提示（ReverseEOL），用于增强冻结LLM的表征能力。ReverseEOL在标准前向嵌入的基础上，额外引入了由反转输入文本生成的反向嵌入。由于反转输入使每个标记能够接触到原始顺序中不可见的上下文，因此生成的反向嵌入能有效提供与原嵌入互补的信息。结果，结合前向和反向嵌入可获得更丰富的最终表示。在STS和MTEB基准上的全面实验表明，ReverseEOL显著提升了多种架构和规模的LLM在现有无训练基线上的性能。广泛的消融实验和分析进一步证实了反转机制的必要性。

summary: Handling toxic retrieval in text-to-audio systems is challenging due to contextual dependencies. Existing strategies (e.g., rephrasing, summarization) risk altering intent or omitting details. We propose a post hoc causal debiasing framework with a sentiment-controlled mediator to preserve semantic relevance while suppressing harmful speech. Our approach is model-agnostic and integrates seamlessly with existing retrieval pipelines. We introduce two variants: Forgive, which re-ranks and filters toxic audio via logit adjustment, and Forget, which generates counterfactual toxic prompts to mitigate harmful retrievals. Experiments show consistent toxicity reduction with minimal loss in retrieval accuracy, improving both safety and reliability.
title: Forgive or forget: Understanding the context of hate in audio retrieval systems
title_translated: 宽恕或遗忘：理解音频检索系统中仇恨内容的上下文
summary_translated: 在文本到音频系统中处理有害检索具有挑战性，因为其存在强烈的上下文依赖性。现有策略（如重述、摘要）可能改变意图或遗漏细节。我们提出一种后处理因果去偏框架，采用情感控制的中介机制，在保持语义相关性的同时抑制有害言论。该方法具有模型无关性，可无缝集成到现有检索流程中。我们提出了两种变体：Forgive，通过逻辑调整重新排序并过滤有害音频；Forget，生成反事实的有害提示以缓解有害检索。实验表明，该方法在几乎不损失检索准确率的情况下实现了持续的毒性降低，提升了系统的安全性和可靠性。

summary: Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) has become a key technology for human--AI interaction. However, code-switching ASR (CS-ASR) remains particularly challenging due to the severe scarcity of multilingual CS speech resources across diverse language pairs. Existing approaches primarily improve CS-ASR performance through synthetic CS speech generation or pair-specific fine-tuning on limited bilingual datasets. Nevertheless, these approaches face an inherent scalability limitation, as support for CS must be developed separately for language pairs whose number grows combinatorially with the number of supported languages. In this work, we investigate whether CS capabilities learned from a limited set of seen language pairs can generalize to unseen language pairs through model merging and domain generalization methods. Our experiments show that merged bilingual CS-ASR models modestly generalize to unseen language pairs, suggesting limited transfer of bilingual CS capabilities across language pairs.
title: Towards Truly Multilingual ASR: Generalizing Code-Switching ASR to Unseen Language Pairs
comment: ICML 2026 Workshop on Machine Learning for Audio
title_translated: 迈向真正多语言语音识别：将代码切换语音识别泛化至未见过的语言对
summary_translated: 自动语音识别（ASR）已成为人机交互的关键技术。然而，由于跨多样化语言对的多语言代码切换语音资源严重匮乏，代码切换ASR（CS-ASR）仍面临巨大挑战。现有方法主要通过合成代码切换语音或在有限双语数据集上进行特定语言对微调来提升CS-ASR性能。然而，这些方法存在固有的可扩展性限制，因为支持每一对语言都需单独开发，而语言对数量随支持语言数呈组合增长。本文研究是否可通过模型融合和领域泛化方法，使从有限已见语言对中学到的CS能力泛化到未见过的语言对。实验表明，融合后的双语CS-ASR模型对未见过的语言对表现出一定的泛化能力，但表明双语CS能力在不同语言对间的迁移能力有限。

summary: While Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) demonstrate remarkable proficiency on complex vision-language tasks, the mechanisms by which they extract query-relevant visual features from complex, noisy contexts remain opaque. In this paper, we present an in-depth interpretability study that uncovers a profound structural property within MLLMs: functional sparsity in cross-modal retrieval. Leveraging a token-level metric termed Retrieval Attention Mass (RAM), we identify and characterize a highly specialized subset of attention heads, referred to as Context-aware Retrieval (CoRe) heads. Across diverse visual domains and model scales, we observe a clear functional division: CoRe heads act as dedicated information extractors, while most other heads distribute attention over broader contextual regions. Causal interventions further demonstrate the necessity of these specialized heads. Ablating only the top 5% of CoRe heads causes significant degradation in multimodal reasoning performance, whereas ablating lower-ranked heads has minimal effect. Moreover, acceleration experiments validate the utility of CoRe heads, showing that leveraging this localized sparsity significantly accelerates inference while maintaining robust task performance. Our findings reveal a structural principle of functional sparsity within MLLMs, refining the current understanding of mechanistic interpretability and laying a theoretical foundation that can inspire future architecture design and model optimization.
title: Mechanistic Insights into Functional Sparsity in Multimodal LLMs via CoRe Heads
title_translated: 通过CoRe头揭示多模态大模型功能稀疏性的机制洞察
summary_translated: 尽管多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）在复杂的视觉-语言任务中表现出卓越能力，但其如何从复杂且嘈杂的上下文中提取与查询相关的视觉特征的机制仍不清晰。本文通过深入的可解释性研究，揭示了MLLMs中一个深刻的结构性特征：跨模态检索中的功能稀疏性。借助一种称为检索注意力质量（RAM）的标记级度量，我们识别并刻画了一类高度专门化的注意力头，称为上下文感知检索（CoRe）头。在多种视觉领域和模型规模下，我们观察到明确的功能分工：CoRe头充当专用信息提取器，而大多数其他头则将注意力分布于更广泛的上下文区域。因果干预进一步证明了这些专用头的必要性：仅剔除前5%的CoRe头就会导致多模态推理性能显著下降，而剔除排名较低的头影响甚微。此外，加速实验验证了CoRe头的实用性，表明利用这种局部稀疏性可显著加速推理过程，同时保持稳健的任务性能。我们的发现揭示了MLLMs中功能稀疏性的结构原则，深化了对机制可解释性的理解，并为未来架构设计和模型优化提供了理论基础。

summary: Large language models have substantially advanced Text-to-SQL systems, yet applying them to enterprise-scale databases remains challenging. Real-world databases often contain large and heterogeneous schemas, incomplete metadata, dialect-specific SQL syntax, and complex analytical questions that are difficult to solve with a single SQL query. To address these challenges, we propose ProSPy, a Profiling-driven SQL--Python agentic framework for enterprise-scale Text-to-SQL. ProSPy structures the reasoning process into four stages: it first extracts fine-grained data evidence through automatic profiling, progressively prunes large schemas into task-relevant contexts, fetches intermediate views through a dialect-agnostic SQL interface, and finally performs flexible downstream analysis with Python. This design combines the efficiency of SQL over large databases with the flexibility of Python-based analysis, while reducing reliance on unreliable metadata and improving robustness across SQL dialects. Experiments on Spider 2.0-Lite and Spider 2.0-Snow show that ProSPy consistently outperforms strong baselines with both open-source and proprietary models, achieving execution accuracies of 60.15% and 60.51% with Claude-4.5-Opus, without majority voting. Further analysis shows that ProSPy is robust to SQL dialect variations and achieves a favorable trade-off between schema recall and precision.
title: ProSPy: A Profiling-Driven SQL-Python Agentic Framework for Enterprise Text-to-SQL
title_translated: ProSPy：一种基于分析驱动的SQL-Python智能体框架，用于企业级文本到SQL
summary_translated: 大型语言模型显著推进了文本到SQL系统的发展，但在企业级数据库中的应用仍面临挑战。现实世界中的数据库通常包含大规模且异构的模式、不完整的元数据、特定方言的SQL语法，以及难以通过单一SQL查询解决的复杂分析问题。为应对这些挑战，我们提出了ProSPy，一种基于分析驱动的企业级文本到SQL智能体框架。ProSPy将推理过程分为四个阶段：首先通过自动分析提取细粒度的数据证据，逐步将大型模式缩减为任务相关的上下文，通过与方言无关的SQL接口获取中间视图，并最终使用Python进行灵活的下游分析。该设计结合了SQL在大规模数据库上的效率与基于Python分析的灵活性，同时减少了对不可靠元数据的依赖，并提升了跨SQL方言的鲁棒性。在Spider 2.0-Lite和Spider 2.0-Snow上的实验表明，ProSPy在使用开源和专有模型时均持续优于强基线，采用Claude-4.5-Opus模型时执行准确率分别达到60.15%和60.51%，且无需多数投票。进一步分析显示，ProSPy对SQL方言变化具有鲁棒性，并在模式召回率与精确率之间实现了良好的权衡。

summary: Prompted knowledge cutoff instructs a large language model (LLM) to act as if information beyond a specified cutoff date were unavailable. However, prior work mainly relies on direct-answer generation, which struggles when post-cutoff knowledge is not explicitly queried but is only causally related to the question. To address this limitation, we propose two recall-based prompting strategies: Self-Recall (SR), which asks the model to restate its cutoff constraint, and Question-Recall (QR), which requires the model to recall question-relevant information valid under the cutoff. Across three existing benchmarks, our methods outperform both direct-answer prompting and conventional step-by-step reasoning baselines, with particularly strong improvements on counterfactual questions. To investigate robustness across different cutoff settings, we further construct the Multi-cutoff Historical Event Benchmark (MHEB), which evaluates the same question under multiple cutoff years. Results show that knowledge cutoff performance varies with cutoff distance, while combining SR and QR consistently yields the best performance.
title: Can LLMs Be Constrained to the Past? Improving Knowledge Cutoff through Recall-Based Prompting
title_translated: 大语言模型能否被约束于过去？通过基于回忆的提示提升知识截止能力
summary_translated: 提示式知识截止指令要求大语言模型（LLM）表现得如同在指定截止日期之后的信息不可用。然而，以往工作主要依赖直接生成答案，当截止日期后的知识未被明确提问但仅与问题存在因果关联时，这种方法表现不佳。为解决这一局限，我们提出两种基于回忆的提示策略：自回忆（SR），要求模型重述其截止约束；问题回忆（QR），要求模型回忆在截止条件下相关的问题信息。在三个现有基准上的实验表明，我们的方法优于直接回答提示和传统逐步推理基线，尤其在反事实问题上提升显著。为进一步研究不同截止设置下的鲁棒性，我们构建了多截止历史事件基准（MHEB），评估同一问题在多个截止年份下的表现。结果表明，知识截止性能随截止距离变化，而结合SR与QR的方法始终表现最佳。

summary: While LLM-based agents excel at individual tasks, effective collaboration with realistic human partners remains challenging. Most of the existing conversation-level collaborative studies lack grounded interaction and behavioral execution, motivating the need for cooperative game environments that enable contextualized and immersive collaboration. To this end, this paper proposes CollabBench, a benchmark for evaluating and training collaborative agents in cooperative games. CollabBench features a Diverse Player Profile Simulation pipeline to model varied players behaviors, and a Collaborative Agentic Training paradigm that unifies reasoning, communication, and action via agentic rollouts, optimized with a hybrid reward balancing task efficiency and affective adaptation. We further extend classic environments to CWAH-MultiPlayer and Cook-MultiPlayer for systematic evaluation under diverse personalities. Experiments with efficiency and affective metrics show that our trained models outperform base models, achieving 19.5% higher efficiency and 24.4% improved affective performance. Further analysis reveals key collaborative limitations of existing models and offers insights for future collaborative training.
title: CollabBench: Benchmarking and Unleashing Collaborative Ability of LLMs with Diverse Players via Proactive Engagement
title_translated: CollabBench：通过主动参与模拟多样化玩家，评测并释放大语言模型的协作能力
summary_translated: 尽管基于大语言模型的智能体在个体任务中表现出色，但与真实人类伙伴进行有效协作仍具挑战性。现有对话级协作研究大多缺乏真实交互和行为执行，因此亟需能够实现情境化和沉浸式协作的合作游戏环境。为此，本文提出CollabBench，一个用于评估和训练合作智能体的基准平台。CollabBench包含多样玩家行为模拟流程以建模不同玩家行为，并采用协作智能体训练范式，通过智能体滚动优化统一推理、沟通与行动，结合混合奖励机制平衡任务效率与情感适应性。我们还扩展经典环境至CWAH-MultiPlayer和Cook-MultiPlayer，以在多样化人格下进行系统评估。效率与情感指标的实验表明，训练模型优于基础模型，效率提升19.5%，情感表现改善24.4%。进一步分析揭示了现有模型在协作方面的关键局限，并为未来协作训练提供了洞见。

summary: Iterative retrieval-reasoning agents have recently shown promise for multimodal long-document question answering. However, most existing systems maintain a single growing context that mixes retrieval traces, observations, and intermediate reasoning. As interactions accumulate, key evidence becomes scattered and diluted, making multi-hop reasoning noisy. We propose MARDoc, a Memory-Aware Refinement Agent framework that decouples long-document QA into three specialized agents: an Explorer for multi-granularity multimodal retrieval, a Refiner for distilling interaction traces into structured evidence and reasoning memories, and a Reflector for checking evidence sufficiency and providing targeted feedback. Across iterations, the agents rely on a dynamically updated structured memory rather than a full accumulated interaction history. This design reduces context noise while preserving answer-critical facts and their logical dependencies. Experiments on MMLongBench-Doc and DocBench show that MARDoc achieves strong results, outperforming same-backbone baselines and demonstrating the effectiveness of structured memory for agentic document QA.
title: MARDoc: A Memory-Aware Refinement Agent Framework for Multimodal Long Document QA
title_translated: MARDoc：一种面向多模态长文档问答的记忆感知精炼智能体框架
summary_translated: 迭代检索-推理智能体最近在多模态长文档问答中展现出潜力。然而，大多数现有系统维护一个不断增长的单一上下文，其中混合了检索轨迹、观察结果和中间推理内容。随着交互积累，关键证据变得分散且稀释，导致多跳推理噪声增加。我们提出MARDoc，一种记忆感知精炼智能体框架，将长文档问答分解为三个专业智能体：探索者负责多粒度多模态检索，精炼者负责将交互轨迹提炼为结构化证据与推理记忆，反思者负责检查证据充分性并提供针对性反馈。在多轮迭代中，智能体依赖动态更新的结构化记忆，而非完整的累积交互历史。该设计在减少上下文噪声的同时保留了答案关键事实及其逻辑依赖关系。在MMLongBench-Doc和DocBench上的实验表明，MARDoc取得优异结果，优于相同骨干网络的基线，验证了结构化记忆在智能体文档问答中的有效性。

summary: Spatial planning maps are central to territorial governance, translating planning objectives, regulations, and spatial strategies into visual forms for decision-making, public communication, and institutional coordination. Their interpretation, however, requires fine-grained visual perception, spatial reasoning, and policy-informed professional judgment, creating major challenges for both human learners and AI systems. With the rapid progress of Vision-Language Models (VLMs), their use in urban planning analysis is gaining attention, yet existing multimodal benchmarks mainly target general visual understanding and overlook the domain-specific cognitive processes of planning practice. To address this gap, we introduce PlanBench-V, the first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating VLMs in spatial planning map interpretation. We first build the Spatial Planning Map Database (SPMD), an expert-annotated dataset of 223 planning maps and 1629 question-answer pairs curated by professional planners, covering diverse geographic regions and cartographic styles. We then propose a theory-informed evaluation framework assessing four progressive capabilities: Perception, Reasoning, Association, and Implementation, corresponding to the cognitive pipeline of planning map interpretation. Extensive experiments across two generations of VLMs show clear progress but persistent limitations. The best 2026 agentic reasoning model, Qwen3.6-Plus, substantially outperforms the best 2025 model, GPT-4o, by 27%. Nevertheless, all models still struggle with implementation-oriented tasks requiring evaluative judgment, policy sensitivity, and constraint-aware decision-making. These findings reveal fundamental limitations of current VLMs in professional planning contexts and highlight the need for domain-adaptive multimodal reasoning frameworks. Code and data are available at https://plangpt.github.io.
title: PlanBench-V: A Spatial Planning Map Benchmark for Vision-Language Models
title_translated: PlanBench-V：面向视觉-语言模型的空间规划地图基准测试
summary_translated: 空间规划地图是领土治理的核心，将规划目标、法规和空间策略转化为可视化形式，用于决策、公众传播和机构协调。然而，其解读需要精细的视觉感知、空间推理以及基于政策的专业判断，这对人类学习者和AI系统都构成重大挑战。随着视觉-语言模型（VLMs）的快速发展，其在城市规划分析中的应用日益受到关注，但现有多模态基准主要针对通用视觉理解，忽视了规划实践中的领域特定认知过程。为填补这一空白，我们引入PlanBench-V，首个全面评估VLMs在空间规划地图解读能力的基准。我们首先构建了专家标注的数据集SPMD，包含223张规划地图和1629个问答对，由专业规划师整理，涵盖多样地理区域和制图风格。随后提出一个理论指导的评估框架，评估四个递进能力：感知、推理、关联与实施，对应规划地图解读的认知流程。在两代VLMs上的广泛实验显示，虽有明显进展但仍存在持续局限。最佳2026年智能体推理模型Qwen3.6-Plus相比最佳2025年模型GPT-4o提升27%。然而，所有模型在需要评价判断、政策敏感性和约束意识决策的实施导向任务中仍表现困难。这些发现揭示了当前VLMs在专业规划场景下的根本局限，并强调了开发领域自适应多模态推理框架的必要性。代码与数据可在https://plangpt.github.io 获取。

summary: Speculative decoding accelerates generation by verifying multiple drafted tokens in a single target-model forward pass, reducing sequential decoding iterations. Model-free variants avoid auxiliary draft models by reusing text and model states already available during generation, but their speedup depends on the reliability of the constructed drafts. We identify two limitations of existing reuse-based methods: lexically anchored retrieval has limited recall under surface-form variation, and deterministic span copying can be brittle when the retrieved context does not uniquely determine the continuation. We propose \emph{AdaPLD}, a training-free method that adaptively improves both retrieval and draft construction. AdaPLD preserves high-precision lexical reuse while using semantic similarity to recover additional reuse opportunities when lexical matching fails. It further constructs branched reuse hypotheses to account for continuation uncertainty, rather than relying on a single copied span. Across diverse benchmarks, AdaPLD reduces target-model forward passes and achieves up to $3.10\times$ decoding speedup.
title: AdaPLD: Adaptive Retrieval and Reuse for Efficient Model-Free Speculative Decoding
title_translated: AdaPLD：用于高效无模型推测解码的自适应检索与复用
summary_translated: 推测解码通过在单次目标模型前向传播中验证多个草稿标记来加速生成，减少序列解码迭代次数。无模型变体通过复用生成过程中已有的文本和模型状态来避免辅助草稿模型，但其加速效果依赖于所构建草稿的可靠性。我们识别出现有基于复用方法的两个局限：基于词法锚定的检索在表面形式变化下召回率有限，而确定性片段复制在检索上下文无法唯一决定后续内容时容易失效。我们提出 	extit{AdaPLD}，一种无需训练的方法，可自适应地改进检索与草稿构建。AdaPLD 保持高精度的词法复用，同时在词法匹配失败时利用语义相似性恢复额外的复用机会。它进一步构建分支复用假设以应对后续不确定性，而非依赖单一复制片段。在多种基准测试中，AdaPLD 减少了目标模型前向传播次数，实现了最高达 $3.10	imes$ 的解码加速。

summary: Long-form narrative QA requires reasoning over evolving story worlds rather than isolated passages: answers may depend on earlier goals, changing character states, social relations, causal triggers, temporal position, and later consequences. Existing retrieval and graph-augmented generation methods improve evidence access, but their units--chunks, entities, relations, summaries, or tool actions--do not directly encode how evidence functions in a story. We introduce Narrative Knowledge Weaver(NKW), a source-grounded framework that aligns textual evidence, atomic facts, canonical graph structure, entity profiles, interactions, episodes, and storylines. At query time, NKW uses text, graph, and narrative tools with post-retrieval reading skills to assemble evidence and audit actor, scope, polarity, state, and temporal constraints. Across STAGE, FairytaleQA, and QuALITY, NKW is strongest on screenplay-level story-world QA while remaining competitive on more passage-centered benchmarks. Ablations, question-type analyses, graph-asset statistics, and case studies show complementary benefits for character, scene, temporal, causal, and narrative-progression reasoning.
title: Narrative Knowledge Weaver: Narrative-Centric Retrieval-Augmented Reasoning for Long-Form Text Understanding
title_translated: 叙事知识编织者：面向长篇文本理解的叙事中心检索增强推理
summary_translated: 长篇叙事问答需要对不断演化的故事情境进行推理，而非孤立段落：答案可能依赖早期目标、变化的角色状态、社会关系、因果触发、时间位置以及后续后果。现有的检索与图增强生成方法虽提升了证据获取能力，但其基本单元——片段、实体、关系、摘要或工具动作——并未直接编码证据在故事中的作用方式。我们提出叙事知识编织者（NKW），一个基于源文本的框架，将文本证据、原子事实、规范图结构、实体档案、互动、情节和叙事线进行对齐。在查询时，NKW 使用文本、图和叙事工具，并结合后检索阅读技能，组装证据并审核角色、范围、极性、状态和时间约束。在 STAGE、FairytaleQA 和 QuALITY 基准上，NKW 在剧本级故事情境问答中表现最强，同时在更侧重段落的基准上仍具竞争力。消融实验、问题类型分析、图资产统计和案例研究显示，角色、场景、时间、因果和叙事进展推理均获互补收益。

summary: Style representation learning is a powerful tool for authorship analysis and modeling writing style, yet the latent nature of learned representations makes them difficult to interpret. Recent work has attempted to explain these representations by generating natural language descriptions with large language models (LLMs) conditioned on input text. However, such descriptions are often prone to the LLM's biases and hallucinations, and they lack an explicit objective and practical utility. In this work, we propose a novel framework for interpreting style representations through style-eliciting prompts: natural language instructions designed to steer LLMs to generate text that reflects specific stylistic attributes. We curate 1,010 distinct style features spanning 26 stylistic categories and construct a dataset by prompting an LLM to generate text conditioned on these features. Using this data, we train a decoder to generate a style prompt from the style representation of the generated text. We evaluate our approach on three tasks: (1) recovering original style prompts from generated text, (2) generating text in the same style using the recovered prompts, and (3) steering LLM outputs to match the style of human-written texts. Experiments demonstrate that our method consistently outperforms strong baselines that directly prompt LLMs with target text, achieving superior performance in both style description and style imitation. These results highlight that style-eliciting prompts can provide a practical and interpretable interface to stylistic information encoded in style representations.
title: Interpreting Style Representations via Style-Eliciting Prompts
title_translated: 通过风格诱导提示解读风格表征
summary_translated: 风格表征学习是作者分析和写作风格建模的强大工具，但其隐式特性使得表征难以解释。近期工作尝试通过大型语言模型（LLM）生成自然语言描述来解释这些表征，但此类描述常受 LLM 偏见和幻觉影响，且缺乏明确目标和实际用途。本文提出一种新框架，通过风格诱导提示（style-eliciting prompts）来解释风格表征：即设计自然语言指令，引导 LLM 生成体现特定风格属性的文本。我们整理了 1,010 个涵盖 26 种风格类别的独特风格特征，并通过提示 LLM 生成基于这些特征的文本构建数据集。利用该数据，我们训练一个解码器，从生成文本的风格表征中还原风格提示。我们在三个任务上评估该方法：(1) 从生成文本中恢复原始风格提示；(2) 使用恢复的提示生成相同风格的文本；(3) 引导 LLM 输出匹配人类写作文本的风格。实验表明，我们的方法持续优于直接使用目标文本提示 LLM 的强基线，在风格描述与风格模仿方面均表现更优。结果表明，风格诱导提示可为风格表征中编码的风格信息提供实用且可解释的接口。

summary: Multi-agent systems built on large language models (LLMs) have become a prevailing paradigm for tackling complex reasoning, planning, and tool-use tasks. The dominant communication protocol in such systems is natural language: agents exchange messages token-by-token, verbalising their internal reasoning so that peers can read, verify, and respond. While convenient and interpretable, this protocol suffers from three structural drawbacks -- high inference cost, irreversible information loss during discretization, and ambiguity/redundancy of natural language. A growing body of work therefore explores an alternative protocol -- latent communication -- in which agents exchange continuous representations (embeddings, hidden states, or KV-caches) directly, bypassing the bottleneck of text generation. This paper presents a unified framework for organising the rapidly expanding literature on latent communication. We analyse existing methods along three orthogonal axes: (1) WHAT information is communicated (Embeddings, Hidden States, KV-Caches, or other continuous state); (2) WHICH sender-receiver alignment is used (latent-space alignment and layer alignment); and (3) HOW the communicated information is fused into the receiver (concatenation, prepending, mathematical operations, cross-attention, or cache restoration). Under this 3-axis framework, we systematically categorise eighteen representative methods proposed between 2024 and 2026, identify five major design patterns, and surface a set of open challenges -- including cross-architecture alignment, security of latent channels, compression for edge deployment, and the relationship between latent communication and latent chain-of-thought. We hope that this framework both lowers the barrier to entry for new researchers and provides a vocabulary for comparing future work.
title: Beyond tokens: a unified framework for latent communication in LLM-based multi-agent systems
title_translated: 超越标记：面向基于大语言模型多智能体系统的潜在通信统一框架
summary_translated: 基于大语言模型（LLMs）的多智能体系统已成为解决复杂推理、规划和工具使用任务的主流范式。此类系统中占主导地位的通信协议是自然语言：智能体逐标记交换消息，将其内部推理显式表达，以便同伴阅读、验证并回应。尽管便捷且可解释，该协议存在三个结构性缺陷——高推理成本、离散化过程中的不可逆信息损失，以及自然语言的模糊性/冗余性。因此，越来越多的研究探索替代协议——潜在通信：智能体直接交换连续表示（嵌入、隐藏状态或 KV 缓存），绕过文本生成瓶颈。本文提出一个统一框架，用于组织快速发展的潜在通信文献。我们沿三个正交维度分析现有方法：(1) 通信的内容（嵌入、隐藏状态、KV 缓存或其他连续状态）；(2) 发送方-接收方对齐方式（潜在空间对齐与层对齐）；(3) 接收方如何融合通信信息（拼接、前置、数学运算、交叉注意力或缓存恢复）。在此三轴框架下，我们系统分类了 2024 至 2026 年提出的十八种代表性方法，识别出五种主要设计模式，并揭示一系列开放挑战——包括跨架构对齐、潜在通道安全性、边缘部署压缩，以及潜在通信与潜在思维链的关系。我们希望该框架既能降低新研究者的入门门槛，也能为未来工作提供比较词汇。

summary: Parametric retrieval augmentation encodes document information into lightweight, document-specific modules such as LoRA adapters, reducing the need to include all evidence as input context. However, it remains unclear how this parameter-side memory interacts with context-side memory stored in the KV cache. We study this interaction in document-level question answering by progressively evicting document key-value states and measuring when a document LoRA contributes beyond the retained context. We find that document LoRA adds little when the KV cache is largely intact, but becomes increasingly useful under aggressive compression, recovering 13-21 ROUGE-L points when no document context remains. The gain is largest when the base model encodes the document, and the adapter is applied only during answer generation, suggesting that document LoRA is better understood as decoding-time parametric memory than as a document encoder. Finally, QA-style supervision produces substantially stronger adapters than raw-context next-token-prediction. These results position document LoRA as a complementary memory channel whose value emerges precisely when context-side evidence is scarce.
title: Rethinking LoRA Memory Through the Lens of KV Cache Compression
title_translated: 从 KV 缓存压缩视角重新思考 LoRA 内存
summary_translated: 参数化检索增强将文档信息编码为轻量级、文档特定的模块（如 LoRA 适配器），减少了将所有证据作为输入上下文的需求。然而，这种参数侧内存与存储在 KV 缓存中的上下文侧内存之间的交互机制尚不清晰。我们通过逐步淘汰文档的键值状态，在文档级问答任务中研究这一交互关系，并测量当文档上下文保留时，文档 LoRA 何时仍能提供额外贡献。我们发现，当 KV 缓存基本完整时，文档 LoRA 贡献甚微；但在激进压缩下，其价值逐渐凸显，当无文档上下文留存时，可恢复 13-21 ROUGE-L 分数。该增益在基础模型编码文档、适配器仅在答案生成阶段应用时最大，表明文档 LoRA 更应被理解为解码时的参数化记忆，而非文档编码器。最后，问答式监督训练产生的适配器显著优于原始上下文的下一个标记预测。这些结果将文档 LoRA 定位为一种互补的记忆通道，其价值恰恰在上下文证据稀缺时才显现。

summary: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models scale foundation models efficiently by activating only a subset of experts for each token, but their large number of expert parameters still makes quantization essential for practical deployment. Unlike dense models, however, MoE models are sensitive to routing instability: small quantization-induced perturbations can change the top-$k$ expert selection, altering the computation path and degrading model quality. We propose Value-and-Structure Routing Alignment for Quantization (VSRAQ), a MoE-specific post-training quantization objective that preserves pre-quantization expert-selection behavior under quantization. VSRAQ combines two complementary objectives that jointly preserve expert-selection behavior: value alignment, which matches routing-relevant logits or scores, and structure alignment, which preserves expert ordering and top-$k$ decision boundaries. By maintaining routing consistency, VSRAQ reduces quantization-induced degradation without introducing any inference-time overhead and can be integrated into existing quantization frameworks. Experiments on recent MoE foundation models show that VSRAQ improves expert-selection consistency and consistently outperforms reconstruction-only and router-aware baselines.
title: Value-and-Structure Alignment for Routing-Consistent Quantization of Mixture-of-Experts Models
title_translated: 面向路由一致性的专家混合模型量化值与结构对齐
summary_translated: 专家混合（MoE）模型通过仅激活每个标记的子集专家来高效扩展基础模型，但其庞大的专家参数仍使量化成为实际部署的关键。然而，与密集模型不同，MoE模型对路由不稳定性敏感：微小的量化扰动可能改变top-$k$专家选择，从而改变计算路径并降低模型质量。我们提出一种针对MoE的后训练量化目标——值与结构路由对齐（VSRAQ），以在量化过程中保持预量化状态下的专家选择行为。VSRAQ结合了两种互补目标：值对齐（匹配与路由相关的logits或得分）和结构对齐（保持专家排序及top-$k$决策边界）。通过维持路由一致性，VSRAQ在不引入任何推理开销的情况下减少了量化带来的性能下降，并可无缝集成到现有量化框架中。在近期MoE基础模型上的实验表明，VSRAQ提升了专家选择的一致性，并持续优于仅重建和路由器感知的基线方法。

summary: Query recommendation in e-commerce search aims to proactively suggest queries that match users' potential interests. However, existing methods mainly optimize query-level relevance, while neglecting whether the retrieved products align with users' downstream preferences. This mismatch often leads to high query click through rates (CTR) but low product conversion rates (CVR). To bridge this gap, we propose QueryAgent-R1, a memory-augmented agentic framework that improves end-to-end alignment via chain-of-retrieval optimization. Our QueryAgent-R1 grounds query generation in real inventory retrieval, allowing the agent to validate and refine queries based on retrieved products. We also design a consistency reward in the agentic reinforcement learning (RL) process to jointly optimize query relevance and downstream engagement. In addition, we construct a memory abstraction module for efficient user profiling. To support offline evaluation, we construct two datasets based on both proprietary industrial data and public datasets, on which QueryAgent-R1 consistently outperforms strong baselines. Moreover, on a large scale production platform, QueryAgent-R1 improves Query CTR by 2.9% and guided CVR by 3.1% in online A/B tests.
title: QueryAgent-R1: Bridging Query Generation and Product Retrieval for E-Commerce Query Recommendation
title_translated: QueryAgent-R1：连接查询生成与商品检索的电商查询推荐
summary_translated: 电商搜索中的查询推荐旨在主动建议符合用户潜在兴趣的查询。然而，现有方法主要优化查询层面的相关性，忽视了检索到的商品是否与用户的下游偏好一致。这种偏差常导致高查询点击率（CTR）但低商品转化率（CVR）。为弥合这一差距，我们提出QueryAgent-R1，一种基于记忆增强的智能体框架，通过检索链优化实现端到端对齐。QueryAgent-R1将查询生成建立在真实库存检索基础上，使智能体能够根据检索结果验证并优化查询。我们还设计了一种在智能体强化学习（RL）过程中使用的统一一致性奖励，联合优化查询相关性与下游参与度。此外，我们构建了一个记忆抽象模块以实现高效的用户画像。为支持离线评估，我们基于自有工业数据和公开数据集构建了两个数据集，QueryAgent-R1在这些数据集上持续优于强基线。此外，在大规模生产平台上，QueryAgent-R1在在线A/B测试中使查询CTR提升2.9%，引导转化率（CVR）提升3.1%。

summary: Real-world text-to-SQL is often under-specified until user phrases are grounded in how the database stores values. Prior work attempts to address this by requiring a semantic layer to specify groundings in advance, but such specifications are often incomplete, especially in expert domains where domain-specific conventions are under-documented. As this leaves multiple grounding hypotheses open for the same SQL part, we introduce GATE (Grouding After Test from Execution), which bootstraps missing groundings from execution feedback. GATE keeps grounding hypotheses open while executing the already grounded parts to obtain observations. Then, only the hypothesis supported by that observation is grounded and stored as a memory entry, recording what was tested and how the open part should be written in SQL. These entries accumulate into execution-grounded memory, allowing later steps to reuse supported groundings. Across real-world and controlled benchmarks, GATE consistently improves over strong baselines, demonstrating that execution can serve not only as validation but also as a bootstrapping mechanism for reusable memory in text-to-SQL.
title: Bootstrapping Semantic Layer from Execution for Text-to-SQL
title_translated: 从执行中自举语义层以实现文本到SQL
summary_translated: 现实世界中的文本到SQL任务通常在用户短语与数据库存储方式未明确关联前处于信息不足状态。先前工作试图通过要求预先定义语义层来指定关联关系，但此类规范往往不完整，尤其在专业领域中，领域特定惯例常缺乏文档记录。这使得同一SQL部分存在多个可能的关联假设。为此，我们提出GATE（执行后从执行中获取关联），通过执行反馈自举缺失的关联。GATE在执行已确定部分的同时保持关联假设开放以获取观察结果；随后，仅由该观察支持的假设被确认并作为记忆条目存储，记录下测试内容以及待写SQL部分的正确形式。这些条目累积形成执行驱动的记忆，使后续步骤可复用已被支持的关联。在真实世界和受控基准测试中，GATE持续优于强基线，证明执行不仅可用于验证，还可作为可复用记忆的自举机制。

summary: Machine-generated text (MGT) attribution aims to identify the specific generator responsible for a given text, thereby providing fine-grained evidence for model accountability and misuse investigation. As new large language models continue to emerge, attribution models must continuously incorporate new generators while preserving their ability to recognize previously seen ones. Prior works have shown that this lifelong MGT attribution setting is challenging, and existing methods often struggle to achieve a stable balance between adapting to new classes and retaining old ones. To address this issue, we propose RidgeFT, a lightweight analytic update framework that does not rely on exemplar replay. RidgeFT trains a task-aware encoder on the initial generator set, stores compact class-wise sufficient statistics when each generator class is first observed, and then freezes the encoder for replay-free closed-form updates. It then suppresses generator-irrelevant variation through covariance calibration, improves representation capacity with fixed random features, and updates new classes through closed-form ridge regression based on class-level sufficient statistics. Across multi-topic evaluations with varying initial generator setups, RidgeFT consistently outperforms baselines. It achieves the best macro-F1 across domains, backbones, and incremental protocols, while also improving both old-class retention and new-class adaptation. These results suggest that feature-stable analytic updates provide a simple yet effective approach to lifelong MGT attribution.
title: When New Generators Arrive: Lifelong Machine-Generated Text Attribution via Ridge Feature Transfer
title_translated: 新生成器到来时：基于岭特征迁移的终身机器生成文本归属识别
summary_translated: 机器生成文本（MGT）归属旨在识别特定文本所对应的生成器，从而为模型问责和滥用调查提供细粒度证据。随着大型语言模型不断涌现，归属模型必须持续整合新生成器，同时保留对已有生成器的识别能力。以往研究表明，这一终身MGT归属设置具有挑战性，现有方法难以在适应新类别与保留旧类别之间取得稳定平衡。为解决此问题，我们提出RidgeFT，一种轻量级解析更新框架，无需依赖示例重放。RidgeFT在初始生成器集合上训练一个任务感知编码器，首次观测到每个生成器类别时存储紧凑的类别级充分统计量，之后冻结编码器以实现无重放的闭式更新。它通过协方差校准抑制无关生成器的变异，利用固定随机特征提升表示能力，并基于类别级充分统计量通过闭式岭回归更新新类别。在多主题、不同初始设置的评估中，RidgeFT持续优于基线，跨领域、骨干网络和增量协议均取得最佳宏F1分数，同时显著提升旧类别保留与新类别适应能力。结果表明，特征稳定的解析更新为终身MGT归属提供了一种简单而有效的方法。

summary: Planning for real-world problems by language models often involves both world and user constraints, which may not be fully specified upfront and are progressively disclosed through interaction. However, existing benchmarks still underexplore adaptive planning under such progressively revealed dual constraints. To address this gap, we introduce AdaPlanBench, a dynamic interactive benchmark for evaluating whether Large Language Model (LLM) agents can adaptively plan and re-plan under progressively revealed world and user constraints. AdaPlanBench is built on 307 household tasks, with a scalable constraint construction pipeline that augments each task with dual constraints. At runtime, agents interact with the environment in a multi-turn protocol where hidden constraints are revealed only when the agent proposes a plan that violates them, requiring iterative plan revision under accumulating feedback. This makes planning challenging, as agents must infer and track constraints from feedback while re-planning effectively. Experiments on ten leading LLMs show that adaptive planning under dual constraints remains challenging, with the best model reaching only 67.75% accuracy. We further observe that performance degrades as more constraints accumulate, with user constraints posing a particularly large challenge and failures often stemming from weaker physical grounding and reduced effectiveness. These results establish AdaPlanBench as a testbed for dual-constrained interactive planning and highlight the challenge of reliable adaptation to dynamically revealed constraints in LLM agents.
title: AdaPlanBench: Evaluating Adaptive Planning in Large Language Model Agents under World and User Constraints
title_translated: AdaPlanBench：在世界与用户约束下评估大语言模型智能体的自适应规划能力
summary_translated: 语言模型在现实世界问题中进行规划时常涉及世界约束和用户约束，这些约束可能无法事先完全明确，而是通过交互逐步揭示。然而，现有基准仍未能充分探索在逐步揭示的双重约束下的自适应规划。为填补这一空白，我们引入AdaPlanBench，一个动态交互式基准，用于评估大语言模型（LLM）智能体在逐步揭示的世界与用户约束下是否具备自适应规划与重规划能力。AdaPlanBench基于307个家庭任务构建，采用可扩展的约束生成管道，为每项任务添加双重约束。运行时，智能体以多轮协议与环境交互，隐藏约束仅在智能体提出违反约束的计划时才被揭示，要求智能体在累积反馈下迭代修正计划。这使得规划极具挑战性，因为智能体必须从反馈中推断并跟踪约束，同时有效重规划。对十种领先LLM的实验表明，在双重约束下的自适应规划依然困难，最优模型准确率仅为67.75%。我们进一步观察到，随着约束数量增加，性能下降，其中用户约束构成特别大的挑战，失败常源于较弱的物理常识基础和较低的有效性。这些结果确立了AdaPlanBench作为双约束交互式规划的测试平台，并突显了LLM智能体在动态揭示约束下可靠适应的挑战。

summary: Recursion enables the generation of hierarchical linguistic structures but imposes substantial processing demands during real-time comprehension. While difficulties with complex syntax have been reported in autism spectrum disorder (ASD), the temporal dynamics of recursive processing remain poorly understood. This study used event-related potentials (ERPs) to examine how Mandarin-speaking children with ASD process two-level recursive locative constructions. Twenty-four children (12 ASD, 12 typically developing, TD) participated in a cross-modal sentence-picture matching task. Neural responses were analyzed across three processing stages associated with structural prediction (P200), semantic integration (N400), and syntactic reanalysis (P600), with mental age controlled. Results revealed a systematic divergence between groups. TD children showed clear P200 and P600 modulation in response to structural mismatch, whereas ASD children exhibited attenuated early differentiation and reduced late reanalysis effects. In contrast, ASD children showed enhanced N400 responses under mismatch conditions, indicating increased semantic integration demands. In addition, the ASD group displayed significantly greater inter-individual variability in hemispheric lateralization, although lateralization strength was not associated with receptive vocabulary performance. These findings support a cascading account in which reduced early predictive engagement in ASD leads to increased integration costs and diminished reanalysis efficiency during recursive processing. More broadly, the results highlight the importance of both temporal processing dynamics and neural variability in understanding language differences in ASD.
title: An ERP Study on Recursive Locative Processing in Mandarin-Speaking Children with Autism
title_translated: 汉语自闭症儿童递归空间结构加工的ERP研究
summary_translated: 递归能力能够生成层级化的语言结构，但在实时理解过程中会带来较大的认知负荷。尽管已有研究报道自闭症谱系障碍（ASD）个体在复杂句法处理方面存在困难，但其递归加工的时间动态机制仍不明确。本研究采用事件相关电位（ERPs）技术，考察了汉语自闭症儿童对两级递归空间结构的加工过程。24名儿童参与实验（12名ASD，12名典型发育TD），完成跨模态句子-图片匹配任务。神经反应在三个与结构预测（P200）、语义整合（N400）和句法重分析（P600）相关的加工阶段进行分析，并控制心理年龄。结果发现两组存在系统性差异：TD儿童在结构不匹配时表现出明显的P200和P600调制，而ASD儿童则早期分化减弱、晚期重分析效应降低；相反，ASD儿童在不匹配条件下表现出增强的N400反应，表明语义整合负担增加。此外，ASD组在半球偏侧化方面表现出显著更高的个体间变异性，但偏侧化强度与接受性词汇能力无关联。这些发现支持一种级联模型，即ASD个体在早期预测参与度降低，导致递归加工中整合成本上升且重分析效率下降。更广泛而言，结果强调了时间加工动态与神经变异性在理解ASD语言差异中的重要性。

summary: The morphological form of a word can often give cues to its meaning, but purely relying on these mappings can lead to overgeneralization in high-stakes domains. In the medical domain, for instance, LLMs can confidently reason about fictitious drugs from their affixes alone (e.g., wugcillin) and generate plausible-looking clinical content. We present a behavioral and mechanistic study of LLM "affix heuristics" in pharmacology. Using fictitious drug names built from real affixes, we show that affix signals alone elicit class-level pharmacological responses. We introduce a framework for identifying whether a model's drug semantics are driven mainly by the affix, the stem, or the drug name as a whole. Applied across 653 drugs, our framework reveals that models often induce drug meaning primarily through affix cues, yet rarely explicitly indicate this reliance, and sometimes incorrectly conflate properties among affix-sharing drugs. Activation patching across models further localizes this behavior to early-mid layers. These findings show that morphological shortcuts pose a subtle but measurable risk to safety.
title: What's in a Name? Morphological Shortcuts by LLMs in Pharmacology
title_translated: 名字里有什么？大语言模型在药学领域中的形态学捷径
summary_translated: 词语的形态形式常常能提示其含义，但仅依赖此类映射可能导致高风险领域中的过度泛化。例如，在医学领域，大语言模型可仅凭词缀自信推断虚构药物（如wugcillin）并生成看似合理的临床内容。本文通过行为与机制研究，探讨了大语言模型在药学中的“词缀启发式”现象。利用由真实词缀构成的虚构药物名称，我们发现仅词缀信号即可引发药物类别层面的药理响应。为此，我们提出一个框架，用于判断模型的药物语义主要由词缀、词干或整个名称驱动。在653种药物上的应用表明，模型通常主要通过词缀线索推断药物意义，却很少明确指出这种依赖，有时还会错误混淆共享词缀的药物属性。跨模型的激活修补分析进一步将该行为定位到早期至中期层。这些发现表明，形态学捷径虽隐蔽但可测量，对安全性构成潜在风险。

summary: The efficacy of continued pre-training for Large Language Models (LLMs) hinges upon hyperparameter configurations, such as learning rate and batch size. However, current practices often rely on heuristics or grid searches, leading to training instability and excessive costs. In this work, we first empirically discover that optimal hyperparameters follow stable and predictable scaling laws throughout the continued pre-training process. Leveraging these insights, we propose a novel framework to establish quantitative relationships between compute budget and optimal hyperparameters for a given checkpoint. Our approach has two stages: (1) \textit{Empirical Law Discovery}, where we train small-scale proxy models to derive functions mapping compute budget to optimal hyperparameters via standard loss-compute scaling laws; and (2) \textit{State-Aware Hyperparameter Prediction}, where we evaluate an initial checkpoint's validation loss and use the inverse scaling law to estimate its \textit{equivalent pre-training compute} -- the compute needed to achieve the same loss from scratch. Combining this with the planned compute budget, we predict optimal hyperparameters for the target run. Empirical results demonstrate that our method reduces the hyperparameter search overhead by up to 90\% while achieving comparable or superior performance relative to baselines. This model-agnostic framework generalizes across architectures, providing a principled and efficient methodology for diverse continued pre-training scenarios starting from any given point.
title: Predictable Scaling Laws of Optimal Hyperparameters for LLM Continued Pre-training
title_translated: 大语言模型持续预训练最优超参数的可预测缩放规律
summary_translated: 大语言模型（LLMs）持续预训练的效果高度依赖于超参数配置，如学习率和批量大小。然而，当前实践多依赖经验法则或网格搜索，常导致训练不稳定且成本过高。本研究首次实证发现，在持续预训练过程中，最优超参数遵循稳定且可预测的缩放规律。基于此，我们提出一种新框架，建立给定检查点下计算预算与最优超参数之间的定量关系。该方法包含两个阶段：(1) 实验规律发现——通过训练小规模代理模型，利用标准损失-计算缩放规律推导出计算预算到最优超参数的映射函数；(2) 状态感知超参数预测——评估初始检查点的验证损失，利用逆缩放规律估计其等效预训练计算量（即从零开始达到相同损失所需的计算量）。结合计划计算预算，预测目标训练运行的最优超参数。实证结果表明，该方法可将超参数搜索开销降低高达90%，同时性能与基线相当或更优。该模型无关框架适用于多种架构，为从任意起点出发的多样化持续预训练场景提供了一种原则性强且高效的解决方案。

summary: Repository-level coding benchmarks face a trade-off between task difficulty and evaluation reliability: tasks that challenge frontier models often involve large codebases with incomplete test coverage, while human review does not scale. We introduce TensorBench, a benchmark of 199 feature-addition and refactoring tasks on an open-source compiler-based tensor framework that extends PyTorch with first-class support for dense and sparse tensors. Tasks cover new sparse formats, dense optimization passes, IR transformations, scheduler changes, runtime components, and high-level numerical operators. TensorBench grades each run by applying the agent's patch and running the framework's test suite, which includes the pre-existing randomized regression tests and any tests the agent adds. For feature-addition tasks, a pass means that the patched repository preserves the tested pre-existing behavior and satisfies the agent-added checks for the requested feature. We evaluate seven coding agents spanning three frontier model families and one open-weight model. Pass rates under this criterion range from $64.8\%$ for the strongest agent to $22.1\%$ for the weakest. Agents pass different subsets of tasks: pairwise Cohen's $κ$ ranges from $-0.07$ to $0.43$, with $κ= 0.05$ for the two strongest agents.
title: TensorBench: Benchmarking Coding Agents on a Compiler-Based Tensor Framework
title_translated: TensorBench：基于编译器框架的编码智能体基准测试
summary_translated: 代码库级别的编码基准测试面临任务难度与评估可靠性之间的权衡：挑战前沿模型的任务往往涉及大型代码库且测试覆盖不全，而人工评审又难以扩展。我们提出了TensorBench，一个针对开源编译器基础张量框架的基准测试，该框架在PyTorch基础上扩展了对密集和稀疏张量的一流支持。共包含199个功能添加与重构任务，涵盖新的稀疏格式、密集优化遍历、中间表示（IR）转换、调度器变更、运行时组件及高层数值算子。TensorBench通过应用智能体的补丁并运行框架的测试套件来评分，该测试套件包括原有随机回归测试以及智能体新增的测试。对于功能添加任务，通过标准意味着补丁后的仓库保持原有测试行为，并满足智能体添加的请求功能检查。我们评估了七种编码智能体，涵盖三种前沿模型家族和一个开源权重模型。在该标准下，通过率从最强智能体的64.8%降至最弱的22.1%。各智能体通过不同任务子集：成对Cohen's κ值范围为-0.07至0.43，其中最强两个智能体间的κ值为0.05。

summary: Mispronunciation Detection and Diagnosis (MDD) has gained increasing importance in computer-assisted language learning and speech technology in recent years. In this paper, we propose a method for constructing statistical graphs that enable models to learn phoneme confusion patterns represented as directed graphs. Furthermore, we introduce a language-specific strategy to capture systematic pronunciation differences across various native language (L1) backgrounds. The effectiveness of our approach is demonstrated through extensive experiments on the L2-ARCTIC benchmark, where it achieves an F1-score of 59.52%, outperforming several competitive baselines.
title: Domain-Aware Mispronunciation Detection and Diagnosis Using Language-Specific Statistical Graphs
title_translated: 基于语言特异性统计图的领域感知发音错误检测与诊断
summary_translated: 近年来，发音错误检测与诊断（MDD）在计算机辅助语言学习和语音技术中日益重要。本文提出一种构建统计图的方法，使模型能够学习以有向图形式表示的音素混淆模式。此外，我们引入一种语言特异性的策略，以捕捉不同母语背景（L1）之间的系统性发音差异。我们在L2-ARCTIC基准上进行了大量实验，验证了该方法的有效性，取得了59.52%的F1分数，优于多个竞争性基线。

summary: Undergraduate research programs such as the Summer Undergraduate Research Fellowship (SURF) at Purdue University receive thousands of applications every year, requiring significant time and effort for program staff to evaluate each submission consistently and within tight timelines. This work-in-progress paper describes the development and initial deployment of a large language model (LLM)-based tool to assist in the evaluation of approximately 1,200 student Statements of Purpose (SoPs) for the SURF 2026 cycle at Purdue University. The workflow utilizes OpenAI GPT models (GPT-4o, GPT-5-mini, and GPT-5.2) and uses a structured rubric across six subcategories, each scored on a 0-3 scale. A few SoPs, graded by program staff, were used to tune the model responses. The model prompt was designed to generate both numerical scores, rationales (including positive and negative aspects) and short excerpts from each submission. Using GPT-5.2, the full batch of 1,200 SoPs was processed in approximately 4.6 hours of compute time, averaging roughly 14 seconds per SoP (with per-SoP timing varying with SoP length, which ranged from 500 to 2,000 words). Notable differences in rubric adherence were observed across model versions, with GPT-5.2 adhering most closely. Disagreement in model scores was more pronounced for lower-scoring submissions. The LLM outputs replicated the role previously played by distributed human graders, providing the program coordinator with scored and rationale-annotated outputs for the entire applicant pool. The program coordinator then reviewed these outputs alongside each applicant's SoP, applying the same downstream office criteria used in prior SURF cycles, to produce a shortlist of strong candidates. This coordinator review was completed in approximately 4 hours, compared to the multi-week coordination effort required in prior program cycles.
title: Using Large Language Models to Support High Volume Application Review for an Undergraduate Research Program
title_translated: 利用大语言模型支持本科生研究项目中的高容量申请评审
summary_translated: 普渡大学的暑期本科生研究奖学金（SURF）项目每年收到数千份申请，需要项目工作人员在紧张的时间内一致地评估每份申请，耗费大量时间和精力。本文描述了一种基于大语言模型（LLM）的工具的开发与初步部署，用于协助评估普渡大学2026年SURF周期中约1,200份学生个人陈述（SoP）。该工作流使用OpenAI GPT模型（GPT-4o、GPT-5-mini和GPT-5.2），并采用包含六个子类别的结构化评分标准，每个子项按0-3分打分。通过少量由项目人员评分的个人陈述对模型输出进行调优。模型提示设计为生成数值评分、理由说明（包括正负方面）以及每份申请的简短摘录。使用GPT-5.2，1,200份个人陈述在约4.6小时计算时间内完成处理，平均每份耗时约14秒（根据个人陈述长度变化，范围为500至2,000词）。不同模型版本在评分标准遵循程度上存在明显差异，其中GPT-5.2最符合标准。低分申请的模型评分分歧更为显著。LLM输出复制了以往由分布式人工评审员承担的角色，为项目协调员提供了整个申请人池的评分及理由标注结果。随后，项目协调员结合每份申请的个人陈述，依据以往SURF周期使用的后续筛选标准，完成了最终候选名单的确定，整个过程仅耗时约4小时，远低于以往多周协调所需时间。

summary: Speech-based mental health screening offers scalable depression detection, yet clinical deployment faces a significant barrier: users' privacy concerns about demographic information exposure. Current techniques struggle to resolve this conflict. Adversarial training often fails against unseen threats, whereas Differential Privacy tends to compromise diagnostic performance by injecting noise across all features. This paper presents InfoShield, which minimizes mutual information between speech representations and sensitive attributes while preserving depression classification accuracy. We identify that standard MINE estimators struggle with sequential speech due to temporal-static misalignment, and introduce TimeAwareMINE with cross-modal attention to align acoustic frames with attribute embeddings. Experiments on the Androids Corpus show InfoShield reduces gender inference from 92.6\% to 55.5\% and age inference from 55.7\% to 30.3\% with limited utility loss (6\% F1 reduction), achieving F1=0.784 compared to prior SOTA's 0.723.
title: InfoShield: Privacy-Preserving Speech Representations for Mental Health Screening via Information-Theoretic Optimization
title_translated: InfoShield：基于信息论优化的隐私保护语音表征用于心理健康筛查
summary_translated: 基于语音的心理健康筛查可实现抑郁症的大规模检测，但临床部署面临重大障碍：用户对暴露人口统计信息的隐私担忧。现有技术难以解决这一矛盾。对抗训练常无法应对未知威胁，而差分隐私则因在所有特征上注入噪声而损害诊断性能。本文提出InfoShield，通过最小化语音表征与敏感属性之间的互信息，同时保持抑郁分类准确性。我们发现标准MINE估计器在处理序列语音时因时间-静态错位而表现不佳，因此引入TimeAwareMINE，采用跨模态注意力机制对齐声学帧与属性嵌入。在Androids语料库上的实验表明，InfoShield将性别推断准确率从92.6%降至55.5%，年龄推断从55.7%降至30.3%，仅造成有限的效用损失（F1下降6%），达到F1=0.784，优于先前最优方法的0.723。

summary: A situated query like "where is Lin Wei?" often encodes more than its literal content: the user may also want to know whether Lin Wei is free, in a good mood, or worth interrupting now. Standard tool-use agents answer the literal question and stop. AURA inserts an inference step between scene perception and tool use that produces an IntentFrame: a structured estimate of the implicit need with a scalar gap score that controls per-query probe budget and tool selection. On a 100-query four-scene implicit-intent benchmark, AURA improves implicit-need coverage over ReAct-style probing (Delta = +0.07, p < 10^-6); three of four scenes are individually significant, the gain reproduces on a second backbone, and a prompt ablation attributes the lift to gap calibration rather than answer memorisation. On factual lookup the controller trades raw accuracy for 82% fewer probes and zero forbidden-tool violations on a privacy-sensitive slice; scope conditions are detailed in Limitations. Code, simulator, and benchmark are released at https://github.com/innovation64/AURA.
title: AURA: Intent-Directed Probing for Implicit-Need Surfacing in Situated LLM Agents
comment: Submitted to EMNLP 2026. Code, simulator, and benchmark: this https URL
title_translated: AURA：面向情境化大语言模型智能体隐性需求发现的意图导向探测
summary_translated: 一个情境化查询如“林伟在哪里？”往往蕴含超出字面意义的信息：用户可能还关心林伟是否空闲、心情如何或是否值得此刻打扰。标准工具使用智能体仅回答字面问题后即停止。AURA在场景感知与工具使用之间插入一个推理步骤，生成IntentFrame——一种结构化的隐性需求估计，包含标量差距分数以控制每查询的探测预算和工具选择。在一个包含四个场景、100个查询的隐性意图基准测试中，AURA在隐性需求覆盖度上优于ReAct式探测（Δ = +0.07，p < 10^-6）；其中三个场景单独具有显著性，该提升在第二个主干模型上重现，提示消融实验表明性能提升源于差距校准而非答案记忆。在事实查询任务中，控制器以牺牲原始准确率为代价，将探测次数减少82%，且在隐私敏感片段中零违规使用禁止工具；具体作用范围详见局限性部分。代码、模拟器和基准已发布于https://github.com/innovation64/AURA。

summary: Role-playing language agents (RPLAs) should play characters whose values and behavior evolve as the story progresses, not maintain a fixed persona. Existing benchmarks measure factual recall at a given chapter, not whether responses align with the character's psychological trajectory, especially in scenarios the source text never explores. We introduce ArcANE (Arc-Aware Narrative Evaluation), an automatically constructed benchmark spanning 17 novels and 80 principal characters. A Character Arc segments the narrative into phases along a psychological axis, and each probe poses the same scenario across phases, spanning both situations within the source text and situations beyond it. Across six models and six context modes, conditioning on the Character Arc tops every other context strategy on every model, and the gap is largest on scenarios outside the source text where retrieval has nothing to find. We further fine-tune open-weight models on the same data to obtain ArcANE-8B/32B, which widen the Arc advantage even more on scenarios outside the source text.
title: ArcANE: Do Role-Playing Language Agents Stay in Character at the Right Time?
title_translated: ArcANE：角色扮演语言智能体是否能在正确时机保持角色一致性？
summary_translated: 角色扮演语言智能体（RPLAs）应随着故事发展而演进其价值观与行为，而非维持固定人格。现有基准仅衡量特定章节的事实回忆能力，未评估响应是否与角色心理轨迹一致，尤其在源文本未涵盖的情境中。我们提出ArcANE（弧-aware叙事评估），一个自动构建的基准，涵盖17部小说与80个主要角色。角色弧线将叙事沿心理轴划分为若干阶段，每个探测问题在各阶段重复呈现，涵盖源文本内与之外的情境。在六种模型与六种上下文模式下，基于角色弧线的条件化在所有模型上均优于其他上下文策略，且在源文本外情境中差距最大，此时检索无可用信息。我们进一步在相同数据上微调开源权重模型，得到ArcANE-8B/32B，使角色弧优势在源文本外情境中进一步扩大。

summary: The development of multilingual Alzheimer's Disease Dementia (AD) detection models presents significant challenges due to the resource-intensive and time-consuming nature of language-specific model training. We propose a novel solution using cross-language training to detect AD in languages beyond those used for model training. This study investigates multilingual deep learning models for detecting AD across different languages and cognitive impairment levels. Using datasets in English, Chinese, Arabic, and Hindi, we developed transformer-based models for binary AD classification. Our approach achieved F1 scores of 82\% across all languages, demonstrating strong cross-linguistic generalization. The rapid inference time (0.5 seconds) supports potential real-time screening applications, while consistent performance across languages indicates feasibility for global deployment.
title: Multilingual Detection of Alzheimer's Disease from Speech: A Cross-Linguistic Transfer Learning Approach
title_translated: 多语言阿尔茨海默病语音检测：一种跨语言迁移学习方法
summary_translated: 开发多语言阿尔茨海默病痴呆（AD）检测模型面临巨大挑战，因其语言特异性模型训练成本高昂且耗时。我们提出一种新颖方案，通过跨语言训练实现对非训练语言的AD检测。本研究探讨了多语言深度学习模型在不同语言和认知损伤水平下的AD检测能力。基于英语、中文、阿拉伯语和印地语的数据集，我们构建了基于Transformer的二分类模型。该方法在所有语言中均取得82%的F1分数，展现出强大的跨语言泛化能力。快速推理时间（0.5秒）支持潜在的实时筛查应用，且各语言间性能稳定，表明具备全球部署可行性。

summary: Despite advances in safety alignment, prompt-rewriting attacks such as persona modulation, fictional framing and persuasion-based reformulation, can bypass safety filters even on frontier models. Existing defenses either rely on non-scalable human curation or white-box optimisation that overfits to specific model internals, leaving aligned models brittle against the very class of adaptive black-box adversaries they will face in deployment. To address this gap, we introduce CHASE (Co-evolutionary Hardening through Adversarial Safety-Escalation), a closed-loop red-blue teaming framework in which a black-box attacker and a safety-aligned defender co-evolve. The attacker is trained via Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) under a multiplicative reward that jointly enforces bypass effectiveness and intent fidelity, while the defender is hardened on the harvested adversarial rewrites through a two-stage GRPO + rejection-sampled SFT pipeline balanced with benign data. Evaluated on BeaverTails and JailbreakBench against five held-out attack families (PAIR, TAP, AutoDAN, PAP, Translation), CHASE cuts mean StrongREJECT score by 43.2\% with 0\% false-refusal on benign prompts. Beyond the headline result, CHASE shows that template-free RL exploration recovers latent attack primitives that transfer across mechanistically distinct attack families, suggesting a path toward LLM safety hardening that generalises beyond the narrow distributions achieved thus far in adversarial training.
title: CHASE: Adversarial Red-Blue Teaming for Improving LLM Safety using Reinforcement Learning
title_translated: CHASE：基于强化学习的对抗性红蓝团队协作以提升大语言模型安全
summary_translated: 尽管在安全对齐方面取得了进展，提示重写攻击（如人格模态、虚构框架和基于说服的重构）仍能绕过前沿模型的安全过滤器。现有防御方法要么依赖不可扩展的人工筛选，要么依赖白盒优化，容易过度拟合特定模型内部结构，导致对部署中将面临的自适应黑盒攻击者缺乏鲁棒性。为弥补这一差距，我们提出CHASE（通过对抗性安全升级实现协同强化），一个闭环红蓝团队框架，其中黑盒攻击者与安全对齐的防御者共同演化。攻击者通过组相对策略优化（GRPO）训练，采用乘法奖励联合强化绕过有效性和意图保真度；防御者则通过两阶段GRPO + 拒绝采样SFT流程，在收集到的对抗性重写样本上进行加固，并平衡良性数据。在BeaverTails和JailbreakBench上针对五类保留攻击家族（PAIR、TAP、AutoDAN、PAP、Translation）的评估显示，CHASE使平均StrongREJECT得分降低43.2%，且良性提示的误拒率为0%。除主要结果外，CHASE还表明无模板强化学习探索能够恢复跨机制差异攻击家族转移的潜在攻击原语，暗示了一条可泛化至当前对抗训练所达狭窄分布之外的大语言模型安全强化路径。

summary: Automatic text summarization has become increasingly important due to the rapid growth of digital textual information. This paper presents a Multi-Model Adaptive Summarization Framework designed to improve the robustness and quality of abstractive text summarization. Relying on a single model often leads to inconsistent summarization quality across articles with varying structures and topics. To address this limitation, the proposed framework integrates multiple fine-tuned transformer-based summarization models and introduces an adaptive selection mechanism. In this framework, each model independently generates a candidate summary for the same input article. The generated summaries are then evaluated using automatic evaluation metrics that capture both lexical similarity and semantic relevance. Based on these scores, the framework selects the highest-quality summary as the final output. The models are fine-tuned and evaluated on the widely used CNN/DailyMail news summarization dataset. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves the highest BERTScore among all compared methods with a score of 88.63%. It also outperforms several LLMs such as GPT3-D2, Falcon-7b, and Mpt-7b, highlighting its effectiveness and robustness. These findings highlight the effectiveness of leveraging multiple transformer-based models within an adaptive selection strategy to improve the quality and robustness of automatic text summarization systems.
title: MASF: A Multi-Model Adaptive Selection Framework for Abstractive Text summarization
title_translated: MASF：用于抽象文本摘要的多模型自适应选择框架
summary_translated: 由于数字文本信息的快速增长，自动文本摘要变得日益重要。本文提出一种多模型自适应摘要框架，旨在提升抽象式文本摘要的鲁棒性和质量。依赖单一模型往往导致在结构和主题各异的文章中摘要质量不一致。为解决此局限，该框架整合多个微调的基于Transformer的摘要模型，并引入自适应选择机制。在此框架中，每个模型独立生成同一输入文章的候选摘要。随后使用自动评估指标（涵盖词汇相似性和语义相关性）对生成的摘要进行评估，并根据得分选择最高质量摘要作为最终输出。模型在广泛使用的CNN/DailyMail新闻摘要数据集上进行微调和评估。实验结果表明，所提框架在所有对比方法中达到最高的BERTScore（88.63%），并优于GPT3-D2、Falcon-7b和Mpt-7b等多个大语言模型，凸显其有效性与鲁棒性。这些发现表明，利用多个基于Transformer的模型结合自适应选择策略，可显著提升自动文本摘要系统的质量和鲁棒性。

summary: Prompt ambiguity is a common source of failure in large language models, but is difficult to localize because it is a latent property of the prompt, while existing attribution methods are designed to explain observable outputs such as logits or generated tokens. We introduce PRIG, a gradient attribution method that uses a probe logit to attribute latent ambiguity to token positions. Specifically, PRIG trains a linear probe to distinguish clear prompts from ambiguous prompts and attributes the probe score to earlier token representations in the residual stream. To enable token-level evaluation, we construct synthetic ambiguity datasets across coding, math, and writing by rewriting one task-critical sentence per prompt, and complement them with a human-written gold benchmark. In this setting, PRIG localizes ambiguous spans substantially better than gradient attribution baselines, achieving 0.840 AUROC on the combined synthetic benchmark and 0.891 AUROC on the gold set. It also outperforms GPT-5.4 on sentence-level ambiguity identification and retains useful signal out-of-domain. These results establish PRIG as a practical tool for identifying which parts of a prompt are ambiguous. More broadly, they suggest that latent prompt properties can be localized through intermediate representations, rather than through output-level attribution.
title: Localizing Prompt Ambiguity in Large Language Models with Probe-Targeted Attribution
title_translated: 通过探针定向归因定位大语言模型中的提示模糊性
summary_translated: 提示模糊性是大语言模型失败的常见原因，但难以定位，因其是提示的潜在属性，而现有归因方法旨在解释可观测输出（如logits或生成的token）。我们提出PRIG，一种使用探针logit进行梯度归因的方法，用于将潜在模糊性归因于标记位置。具体而言，PRIG训练一个线性探针以区分清晰提示与模糊提示，并将探针得分归因于残差流中更早的标记表示。为支持细粒度评估，我们在编码、数学和写作领域构建了合成模糊数据集，通过重写每个提示中的一个关键句子，并补充人工编写的黄金基准。在此设置下，PRIG在合成基准上的综合AUROC达到0.840，在黄金集上达到0.891，显著优于梯度归因基线。它还在句子级模糊识别上优于GPT-5.4，并在域外保持有用信号。这些结果确立了PRIG作为识别提示中模糊部分的实用工具。更广泛地，它们表明可通过中间表示而非输出层面归因来定位提示的潜在属性。

summary: Coreference resolution is a core NLP task, having a broad range of downstream applications, e.g.~machine translation, question answering, document summarization, etc. While the task is well-studied in English, comparatively less attention is dedicated to coreference resolution in other languages, especially low-resource ones. To mitigate this gap, we propose a novel coreference resolution pipeline that harnesses machine translation (MT) from English to a target low-resource language, to generate or expand training data. To automatically validate the quality of the translated samples, we back-translate the samples and assess the similarity with the original English samples via cosine similarity in the latent space of a BERT model. The resulting similarity scores are integrated into the loss function to weight training samples according to their MT cycle consistency. Extensive experiments on four low-resource languages show that our pipeline brings significant performance gains in coreference resolution. Moreover, our pipeline enables accurate coreference resolution in languages where no previous corpora were available.
title: Multilingual Coreference Resolution via Cycle-Consistent Machine Translation
title_translated: 通过循环一致性机器翻译实现多语言共指消解
summary_translated: 共指消解是自然语言处理中的核心任务，具有广泛下游应用，如机器翻译、问答系统、文档摘要等。尽管英语中的共指消解研究较为充分，其他语言尤其是低资源语言的研究相对较少。为缓解这一差距，我们提出一种新颖的共指消解流水线，利用从英语到目标低资源语言的机器翻译（MT）生成或扩充训练数据。为自动验证翻译样本的质量，我们对样本进行反向翻译，并通过BERT模型潜空间中的余弦相似度评估其与原始英文样本的相似性。所得相似度分数被集成到损失函数中，按MT循环一致性权重训练样本。在四种低资源语言上的大量实验表明，该流水线显著提升了共指消解性能。此外，该流水线使得此前无可用语料库的语言也能实现准确的共指消解。

summary: When a text is translated, does the translation retain the complexity of the original? We introduce ComplexityMT, a new challenge for assessing how text complexity and machine translation interact with and influence each other, using the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFR) levels as the measure of text complexity. Across six languages, including Arabic, Dutch, English, French, Hindi, and Russian, we evaluate three open-weight models, one closed model, and a commercial machine translation system on two tasks: i) correlation of CEFR with translation difficulty, and ii) shifts in CEFR levels of the source texts. Our experiments show that higher CEFR levels make texts more difficult to translate, and that machine translation shifts the CEFR level of the target text compared to the original source, for most languages. These findings provide new insights for researchers and practitioners working on multilingual pedagogical content generation and machine translation difficulty estimation.
title: ComplexityMT: Benchmarking the Interaction Between Text Complexity and Machine Translation
title_translated: ComplexityMT：评测文本复杂性与机器翻译交互关系的基准
summary_translated: 当文本被翻译时，译文是否保留了原文的复杂性？我们引入ComplexityMT，一项新挑战，用于评估文本复杂性与机器翻译之间的相互作用及其影响，采用欧洲语言共同参考框架（CEFR）等级作为文本复杂性的衡量标准。在包括阿拉伯语、荷兰语、英语、法语、印地语和俄语在内的六种语言上，我们评估了三种开源模型、一个闭源模型以及一个商用机器翻译系统在两个任务上的表现：i）CEFR等级与翻译难度的相关性；ii）源文本CEFR等级在目标文本中的变化。实验结果表明，更高的CEFR等级使文本更难翻译，且大多数语言中机器翻译会导致目标文本的CEFR等级相较于源文本发生偏移。这些发现为多语言教育内容生成和机器翻译难度估计领域的研究人员与实践者提供了新见解。

summary: Real-world data spans tables, documents, and semi-structured files with implicit semantics. Querying this data requires integrating evidence across inconsistent schemas and formats, yet existing approaches either demand costly manual engineering or bypass structure entirely. We present a system that automatically discovers an executable schema from raw multi-source data and uses it as a shared contract for knowledge graph construction and query-time retrieval.
